task_completion.description: Navigate to the inventory requests list or module.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the existing inventory request for Surgical Masks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific request document 'inv_req_masks_001' or the existing request for 'Surgical Masks' is visible and selected.

task_completion.description: Open the existing request in edit mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows the existing request (inv_req_masks_001) in an editable state, confirming a new request is not being created.
The quantity field for the Surgical Masks request displays '500' instead of the initial '100'.

task_completion.description: Save the updated request and ensure the status remains 'Requested'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save confirmation is visible, or the saved document view shows the quantity as 500 and the status clearly marked as 'Requested'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the application's GUI (buttons, forms, menus) rather than terminal commands, API calls, or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify that a new request was not created.
integrity.visual_evidence: The document ID being edited is 'inv_req_masks_001'. There should be no evidence of a 'New Request' or 'Create' form being submitted for Surgical Masks.

integrity.description: Verify the results are from the actual inventory application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots display the actual inventory/ERP software interface, not a text editor or a mocked-up webpage.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target inventory request document has ID 'inv_req_masks_001' and is for the item 'Surgical Masks'. The initial quantity was 100. The expected target quantity is 500. The expected status is 'Requested'. The agent must modify the existing document ('inv_req_masks_001') and not create a new one.
